strategic_profile.executive_summary: Creighton University is a highly respected, mid-sized private Jesuit research university. It is uniquely structured to support aspiring healthcare and business professionals, offering robust direct-entry pipelines to its own medical, dental, law, and pharmacy schools.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Guaranteed admission pipelines via the Pre-Professional Scholars Program.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Highly attractive for ambitious pre-med, pre-dental, and pre-law students looking to bypass the brutal professional school application gauntlet.

analyst_takeaways.insight: High sticker price ($67,000+ COA) offset by 99% of students receiving institutional aid.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students should apply early (by Dec 1) to maximize access to substantial merit scholarships like the Scott and Harper programs.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Median 10-year earnings of $81,178 and a 98% placement rate.
analyst_takeaways.implication: The university provides an exceptionally strong Return on Investment, particularly for Business and Nursing graduates.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Urban campus in Omaha with Division I Big East athletics.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Provides a 'big school' sports environment and urban internship access while maintaining small class sizes and a close-knit community.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Community-oriented, Jesuit-influenced culture with a strong emphasis on service. Greek life is active (35% participation), and school spirit is heavily driven by Division I Big East basketball.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Self-contained urban campus within walking distance of downtown Omaha and the Old Market district, providing easy access to corporate internships and clinical sites.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: 82% of undergraduates complete at least one internship or research project before graduation. Strong undergraduate research presence in health sciences.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional for most programs. Test scores are considered if submitted, but applicants will not be penalized if they choose to withhold them.

gpa.notes: GPA data reflects weighted high school averages of admitted students.

testing.policy_note: 41% of students submit ACT scores, and 10% submit SAT scores.

colleges.name: College of Arts and Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for pre-med and pre-law students seeking a strong liberal arts foundation and engagement with the Magis Core Curriculum.

majors.name: Biology
majors.notes: Highly popular major due to Creighton's strong medical school placements.

colleges.name: Heider College of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Best for ethically-minded aspiring corporate leaders who want to leverage Omaha's Fortune 500 network.

majors.name: Finance
majors.notes: Highly regarded regional business program with exceptional employment outcomes.

colleges.name: College of Nursing
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: For decisive students who know they want to become RNs. Clinical rotations are guaranteed.
colleges.student_archetype: Compassionate, scientifically capable future healthcare providers.

majors.name: Nursing
majors.special_requirements: Requires a minimum of a C in high school chemistry.
majors.notes: Direct entry program is highly competitive. Offers a 12-month Accelerated BSN (ABSN) for post-baccalaureate students.

application_deadlines.notes: Non-binding Early Action. Yields priority scholarship consideration.
Rolling admissions process with decisions typically released beginning in early winter.

supplemental_requirements.details: Personal statement required via the Common App or Creighton App.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Focus on cura personalis; applicants displaying strong commitment to community service and ethics are favored.

major_selection_tactics: Applying undecided or to Arts & Sciences is generally less competitive than applying directly to the College of Nursing.
Pre-health applicants should carefully highlight sustained clinical volunteering or shadowing in their applications to be competitive for the Pre-Professional Scholars Program.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Creighton does not heavily restrict applicants from selecting alternate majors, though internal transfer into Nursing is exceedingly difficult.

scholarships.name: Scott Scholars
scholarships.benefits: Covers total cost of attendance; highly selective for Heider College of Business students.

scholarships.name: Harper Nursing Scholars
scholarships.benefits: Significant tuition discount for elite Nursing applicants.

scholarships.name: Ignatian Merit Scholars
scholarships.benefits: Aims to help low- and middle-income students access a Jesuit education via donor matching.

scholarships.name: Pre-Professional Dental Scholars
scholarships.benefits: Guarantees admission to Creighton Dental School and provides a baseline scholarship.

scholarships.name: Grace Keenan Endowment
scholarships.benefits: For incoming freshmen intending to major or minor in visual or performing arts.

ap_policy.general_rule: Requires scores of 4 or 5 depending on the department.
exceptions: AP credit is not accepted for Biology and Chemistry prerequisites for the Pre-Professional Dental Scholars program.
ap_policy.usage: Awards up to a maximum of 32 credit hours.

ib_policy.general_rule: Requires scores of 5 or higher on Higher Level (HL) exams. No credit is awarded for Standard Level tests.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Credit is not awarded for remedial high school equivalencies.

what_it_takes: A strong academic profile with robust high school rigor.
Demonstrated commitment to service and ethical leadership.
essay_tips: Highlight specific ties to Jesuit values and the 'cura personalis' philosophy.
Clearly articulate professional goals, especially for pre-health and business applicants.
red_flags: Lack of demonstrated service or community engagement.
Poor grades in core STEM courses for prospective Nursing/Pre-Med students.
insights: Creighton's environment is notoriously supportive; faculty go out of their way to assist students via programs like the EDGE academic coaching center.
